name: Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop from El Palau d'Anglesola
name_original: Estanque de Ivars y Vila-sana desde El Palau de Anglesola — circular por el Pla de Urgell
description: This easy 15.0-mile (24.2 km) touring cycling route circles the Ivars Pond and Vila-sana through the flat Pla de Urgell region.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around El Palau D'Anglesola are characterized by the generally flat landscape of the L'Urgell plains in Lleida province, Catalonia. The region features an extensive network of dedicated paths, often following the Segre river and the historic Urgell Canal. Notable natural features include the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond, providing scenic views and opportunities for birdwatching. This terrain offers accessible and enjoyable cycling for various skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around El Palau D'Anglesola The most popular touring cycling route is Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop from El Palau d'Anglesola, a 15.0 miles (24.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the significant Ivars and Vila-sana Pond, offering views of local fauna. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig — circular by the Pla de Urgell, a moderate 16.8 miles (27.1 km) path. This circular route provides expansive views across the flat agricultural landscape of the Pla d'Urgell. Local touring cyclists also love the Bell-lloc d'Urgell – Fita Alta Summit loop from Mollerussa, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail leading towards a summit, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around El Palau D'Anglesola is defined by the flat plains, the Urgell Canal, and the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes. The routes in El Palau D'Anglesola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Palau D'Anglesola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling around El Palau d'Anglesola?
A: The terrain around El Palau d'Anglesola is predominantly flat, characteristic of the L'Urgell plains in Lleida province. This makes for accessible and enjoyable cycling, with many routes following the picturesque Segre river and the historic Urgell Canal.

Q: Are there many car-free cycling options in the area?
A: Yes, the region around El Palau d'Anglesola is excellent for safe cycling, offering a remarkable 105 dedicated itineraries designed to avoid car traffic. This extensive network provides a peaceful and secure environment for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 30 easy touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963917376/ivars-and-vila-sana-pond-loop-from-el-palau-d-anglesola" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond Loop from El Palau d'Anglesola&lt;/a&gt;, which is 24.2 km long and offers beautiful views of the pond and local fauna.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural feature ideal for birdwatching. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4591004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt del Duran Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.5-meter cascade along the Urgell Canal, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt;, which has picnic areas and a small pond.

Q: Are there any historical points of interest to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The region has historical significance, including the route of the old "beet train" (tren de la remolatxa) along the Urgell Canal. You can also appreciate architectural sites like the neoclassical Sant Joan Baptista Church and the 16th-century stately home of Cal Massot in El Palau d'Anglesola.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. A notable moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962991750/la-sierra-de-mollerussa-vilanova-de-bellpuig-circular-by-the-pla-de-urgell" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra de Mollerussa - Vilanova de Bellpuig — circular by the Pla de Urgell&lt;/a&gt;, covering 27.1 km and offering expansive views of the agricultural landscape.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Palau d'Anglesola?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Palau d'Anglesola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat, accessible terrain and the scenic paths along the Urgell Canal and around the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While the region is generally flat, there are routes that offer more challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bell-lloc d'Urgell – Fita Alta Summit loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.3 km trail that includes a climb towards a summit.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the area features several bodies of water. Besides the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ivars and Vila-sana Pond&lt;/a&gt;, you can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassella Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Mollerussa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and various irrigation ponds along the Canal d'Urgell.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Palau d'Anglesola?
A: The generally flat landscape makes touring cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any longer touring routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Martí de Maldà – Bellpuig Castle loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 58.4 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20156609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Third Canal of Urgell Path – Church of Sant Martí de Maldà loop from Mollerussa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.6 km.

Q: Are there facilities like picnic areas along the routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147606" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Park&lt;/a&gt; is an idyllic spot perfect for relaxation and family outings, featuring picnic areas, benches, tables, and barbecues, all shaded by trees.
tips: In the Pla de Urgell region we find this small town. Called Utxafava, until 1930. Its beautiful white church stands out. In the municipality of Vila-sana, there is the largest pond in surface area in all of Catalonia.

Good views of the pond. We can go around it to observe birds and/or a beautiful sunset. So it is recommended to go with binoculars, since we will be able to observe the fauna from several other viewpoints that we will find if we go around the pond.

In the heart of the village of El Palau de Anglesola stands this magnificent 19th-century Baroque church. Besides the church, visitors can also admire the charming old streets that wind through the village.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route around Ivars Pond and Vila-sana is approximately 24 km long. Most cyclists can expect to complete it in about 1 hour and 54 minutes, making it a pleasant ride for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: Is this cycling trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered easy and is highly suitable for beginners and families. The terrain is largely flat, and the route is designed for a relaxed, 'slow tourism' experience, making it accessible for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route through the Pla de Urgell?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly flat, characteristic of the 'Pla de Urgell' region, which means 'plain'. You'll cycle through expansive open farmland and fruit orchards, mostly on paved surfaces. While generally level, there might be very gentle, gradual ascents offering elevated views of the plain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the cycling route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond, Catalonia's largest inland lake and an oasis rich in fauna and flora. You'll also pass by the Church of Vila-sana and the Church of El Palau d'Anglesola. The route also offers views of the historic Canal d'Urgell and peaceful agricultural landscapes.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see, especially at Ivars Pond?
faqs.answer: The Ivars and Vila-sana Pond is a significant wetland, home to over 210 species of water birds. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching, with observation points available along the route. You'll likely see various waterfowl and other wetland species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures for cycling and excellent opportunities for birdwatching at the pond. Summer can be warm in the plains, while winter might be cooler.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in El Palau d'Anglesola?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, El Palau d'Anglesola is the starting point for this circular route. As a small town, you should be able to find convenient street parking or designated public parking areas within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Via cicloturística InterCatalunya - Etapa 01, the Estany d'Ivars i Vila-sana trail itself, and parts of the Camí Català per Lleida and Camí Ignasià Català 3: Palau d'Anglesola - Verdú.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on cycling routes in rural areas like the Pla de Urgell, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially around the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond due to its protected wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb local fauna or other cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle around Ivars Pond?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or access the Ivars and Vila-sana Pond area. It is a publicly accessible natural space, though visitors are encouraged to respect the environment and follow any local regulations for protected areas.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or picnic areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around Ivars and Vila-sana Pond is well-equipped for visitors. You'll find observation points, picnic areas, and viewpoints, including those from Cal Sinén, the visitor center. The nearby villages of Vila-sana and El Palau d'Anglesola may also offer cafes or other services.
